name: the grouping ties each rule to the tool whose execution produced the report, so the finding bubbles up to its enforcing function
command: printf '%s' '{"version":"2.1.0","runs":[{"tool":{"driver":{"name":"gitleaks"}},"results":[{"ruleId":"aws-key","level":"error","message":{"text":"secret"}}]}]}' > a.sarif && bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/rubric/group-findings-by-rule.sh" --dir . --json 2>/dev/null | node -e 'const j=JSON.parse(require("fs").readFileSync(0,"utf8"));process.exit(j.rules["gitleaks/aws-key"].tools[0]==="gitleaks"?0:1)' && echo rule-tied-to-tool 1>&2
